page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 4-core AWS Graviton processor utilizing the ARM64 architecture. Operating at 2.5 GHz, the Neoverse-N1 CPU features 64 KB L1D cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and includes 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance in multi-core and single-core stress-ng CPU tests, alongside average memory bandwidth for large block reads and writes, measuring 69.63 GB/sec and 33.78 GB/sec respectively. However, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak at 235.28 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks show lower efficiency, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the weak to poor ranges. Passmark database operations perform at an average level, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are positioned in the bottom 25%.

The im4gn.xlarge offers high local storage density relative to its compute and memory footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for applications requiring high-capacity, low-latency local NVMe storage without needing massive CPU resources. The primary trade-off is its modest ARM64 compute performance and weaker memory bandwidth in small-block operations. This profile is best suited for storage-intensive workloads, local database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose applications with high I/O requirements.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated ARM64 vCPUs powered by an AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory and a substantial 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities in multi-core CPU stress tests and large-block memory bandwidth, though small-block memory bandwidth and cryptographic operations are weaker. This instance delivers high local storage density, presenting a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for I/O-heavy workloads that do not demand extreme compute power. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, and storage-intensive applications requiring low-latency local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 4 Graviton vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for I/O-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for I/O-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated cores
1875 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
16.0 GB of system memory and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in database operations and large-block memory transfers
Optimized for storage-intensive workloads, local databases, and caching layers
